comment: Fixed corpus for real-embedding fixtures. 32 documents across four realistic domains (docs, support tickets, engineering notes, paper abstracts) with metadata used by adapter tests; 8 queries with expected-domain hints for sanity assertions. Do not reorder entries: fixture row indices are load-bearing.

documents.text: Install the package with pip or build from source with maturin. The extension module requires a Rust toolchain and Python 3.9 or newer.
Indexes persist as directory bundles containing a manifest with sha256 checksums. Loads verify every artifact before any vector state is read.
Create an index, add vectors as a two-dimensional float32 array, and search with a batch of queries to receive scores and row identifiers.
Metadata filters compute an allowlist before search, so top-k results are ranked within the filtered subset rather than filtered afterwards.
Deleting a record removes its external identifier from the map. Allowlist searches that reference deleted identifiers return a clear error.
Back up a store by copying the directory while no writer holds the lock, then verify the copy with the command line tool before relying on it.
The library targets small devices. A Raspberry Pi class ARM64 board can serve embedded retrieval workloads without a running database server.
The recommended planning limit is around one hundred thousand rows per store directory based on measured load and query timings.
Users are stuck in a single sign-on redirect loop after the identity provider certificate rotated. Login bounces back to the provider endlessly.
Password reset emails arrive more than an hour late for accounts on the legacy domain, and the reset links are sometimes already expired.
A customer reports a duplicate charge on their corporate card for the June invoice. Two identical transactions posted forty seconds apart.
The downloadable invoice PDF shows the previous company address even though the billing profile was updated at the start of the quarter.
Search results in the dashboard return stale documents that were deleted last week. Reindexing does not appear to remove them.
Push notifications never arrive on newly enrolled phones until the user signs out and back in. Android and iOS are both affected.
Scheduled CSV exports silently stopped running after the timezone change. No error is logged and the export history shows nothing.
Webhook deliveries are retried forever against an endpoint that returns a permanent client error, flooding the customer's logs.
Root caused the connection pool exhaustion: a background job opens a new connection per row instead of reusing one from the pool.
The worker pods were OOMKilled because the memory limit did not account for the sidecar. Raised limits and added an alert on restarts.
Flaky test traced to a shared temporary directory raced by two suites. Fixed by giving each test process its own scratch path.
Borrow checker fight resolved by restructuring the cache so the read path takes a shared reference and mutation happens in a commit step.
Decision from the platform sync: we standardize on trunk-based development with feature flags and drop long-lived release branches.
Postmortem: the cache stampede after the deploy was caused by all nodes expiring keys simultaneously. Added jitter to the TTLs.
Profiling showed most query latency in JSON serialization, not the database. Switching the hot endpoint to a binary format halved p99.
Rotated the signing keys and moved verification to a fail-closed check at load time so tampered artifacts refuse to deserialize.
Dense passage retrieval learns dual encoders that embed queries and passages, outperforming sparse term matching on open-domain questions.
Approximate nearest neighbor search over billions of vectors combines inverted file partitions with product quantization to trade recall for speed.
Scaling laws suggest compute-optimal language model training balances parameter count against training tokens rather than maximizing size.
Quantized low-rank adaptation fine-tunes large language models on a single GPU by backpropagating through frozen four-bit weights.
A survey of vector database systems compares index structures, filtering strategies, and consistency models for embedding retrieval workloads.
Disk-resident graph indexes for nearest neighbor search reduce memory footprint by an order of magnitude while preserving high recall.
Hybrid retrieval fuses lexical and dense scores with reciprocal rank fusion, improving robustness when either signal fails alone.
Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards elicits multi-step reasoning in language models without supervised chain-of-thought data.

queries.text: customers being billed twice for the same invoice
login loop with single sign-on after certificate change
why is my database connection pool running out
pods keep getting killed for using too much memory
efficient fine-tuning of large language models on one GPU
vector database index structures for nearest neighbor search
how do I persist an index to disk and verify it
running retrieval on a Raspberry Pi without a server
